summary: Auth, Route Guards, and API Client Layer
description:
Complete the Phase 4 auth and scaffold milestone. The Next.js 16 App Router project is already scaffolded (NX workspace, providers, shadcn.json, stub role pages). This epic implements the remaining deliverables: a functional login page wired to the backend JWT credential flow, NextAuth silent refresh, role-aware middleware with per-role route guards, separate root layouts for each role, a configured API client singleton that attaches session tokens, and Zod schemas covering all API request/response shapes.

Key paths: <apps/web/app/(auth)/login>, <apps/web/middleware.ts>, <apps/web/app/candidate/layout.tsx>, <apps/web/app/marketer/layout.tsx>, <apps/web/app/supporter/layout.tsx>, <apps/web/app/admin/layout.tsx>, <apps/web/lib/api>, <apps/web/lib/schemas>, <features/auth/src/lib/auth>
functionality: log in with their credentials, be redirected to their role-specific dashboard, and have the app transparently maintain a valid session
benefit: all four role experiences depend on working auth and session management from day one of frontend development

stories.summary: Implement login page UI and NextAuth credential flow
stories.description:
Replace the login page placeholder with a functional email/password form built in Tailwind CSS. Wire the form to NextAuth's CredentialsProvider, which calls the backend POST /auth/login endpoint. On success, NextAuth stores the access token, refresh token, role, and expiry in the JWT session cookie. On failure, display an inline error. The submit button shows a loading state during the request.

Backend endpoint: POST http://localhost:8080/auth/login
Request body: { email, password }
Response: { accessToken, refreshToken, role, expiresIn } — role is lowercase (e.g. 'candidate')

Key paths: <apps/web/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx>, <features/auth/src/lib/auth/index.ts>, <features/auth/src/lib/types/next-auth.d.ts>

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Valid credentials log the user in
acceptanceCriteria.given: a user enters a registered email and correct password on the login page
acceptanceCriteria.then: NextAuth calls the backend, receives tokens, stores them in the JWT session cookie, and the user is redirected to their role home

acceptanceCriteria.given: a user enters an incorrect password
acceptanceCriteria.then: the backend returns 401, the login page shows 'Invalid email or password', and no redirect occurs

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Already-authenticated user is redirected away from login
acceptanceCriteria.given: a user with an active session navigates to /login
acceptanceCriteria.then: the middleware redirects them to their role home without showing the login form

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Submit button is disabled while request is in flight
acceptanceCriteria.given: the login form has been submitted
acceptanceCriteria.then: the submit button is disabled and shows a loading label

tasks.summary: Add Role type and extend NextAuth User and JWT interfaces
tasks.description: In <features/auth/src/lib/types/next-auth.d.ts>, add `type Role = 'candidate' | 'marketer' | 'supporter' | 'admin'`. Extend the `User` interface with `role: Role`, `jwtToken`, `refreshToken`, `expiration`. Extend the `JWT` interface with the same fields plus `expiration` as an epoch timestamp.

tasks.summary: Wire CredentialsProvider authorize to backend POST /auth/login
tasks.description: In <features/auth/src/lib/auth/index.ts>, implement the `authorize` callback. Call `fetch(process.env.AUTH_API_URL + '/auth/login')` with the email and password credentials. On a 2xx response, return a User object with jwtToken, refreshToken, role, and expiration. On failure, return null. Add AUTH_API_URL to .env and .env.example.

tasks.summary: Implement silent token refresh in the JWT callback
tasks.description: In the NextAuth `jwt` callback, store `token.expiration` as an epoch timestamp (Date.now()/1000 + expiresIn). On each callback invocation, if the token expires within the next 60 seconds, call the backend POST /auth/refresh with token.refreshToken, update token.jwtToken and token.expiration with the response, and rotate token.refreshToken with the new value returned by the backend.

tasks.summary: Build login form UI in Tailwind CSS
tasks.description: Replace <apps/web/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx> with a 'use client' component. Render an email input, a password input, and a submit button with loading state. Call `signIn('credentials', { redirect: false })` on submit. Display the returned error (if any) as an inline alert. On success navigate to '/' — the middleware handles the role redirect.

stories.summary: Implement role-aware middleware and per-role root layouts
stories.description:
Create the Next.js middleware that enforces authentication and role-based routing for all non-API routes. Use NextAuth(authConfig).auth to read the JWT session cookie at the edge. Add four separate root layouts (one per role) with role-specific navigation links and a sign-out server action.

Role routing rules:
- /login → redirect to role home if already authenticated
- / → redirect to role home if authenticated, else to /login
- /candidate/* → require role 'candidate'; mismatch → redirect to own role home
- /marketer/* → require role 'marketer'
- /supporter/* → require role 'supporter'
- /admin/* → require role 'admin'
- Unauthenticated on any protected path → /login
- /api/* → pass through

Key paths: <apps/web/middleware.ts>, <apps/web/app/candidate/layout.tsx>, <apps/web/app/marketer/layout.tsx>, <apps/web/app/supporter/layout.tsx>, <apps/web/app/admin/layout.tsx>, <features/auth/src/lib/auth/auth.config.ts>

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Unauthenticated user is redirected to login
acceptanceCriteria.given: a user with no session navigates to /marketer/processes

acceptanceCriteria.then: the user is redirected to /candidate (their role home)

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Each role sees its own navigation
acceptanceCriteria.given: a marketer logs in and navigates to /marketer/processes
acceptanceCriteria.then: the layout shows marketer-specific navigation links and the user's email

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Sign-out from any layout clears the session and redirects to login
acceptanceCriteria.given: an authenticated user clicks sign out from any role layout
acceptanceCriteria.then: the NextAuth session is cleared and the user is redirected to /login

tasks.summary: Update authConfig signIn page and simplify authorized callback
tasks.description: In <features/auth/src/lib/auth/auth.config.ts>, change pages.signIn from '/auth' to '/login'. Simplify the authorized callback to return true (routing logic moves to the custom middleware wrapper).

tasks.summary: Create middleware.ts with edge-compatible role routing
tasks.description: Create <apps/web/middleware.ts>. Import `NextAuth` and `authConfig`. Call `const { auth } = NextAuth(authConfig)` to get a session-aware wrapper. Export `auth((req) => { ... })` as default. Inside the callback read `req.auth?.user?.role`, implement the role routing rules, and return appropriate NextResponse.redirect() calls. Match with `export const config = { matcher: ['/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico).*)'] }`.

tasks.summary: Create candidate layout with navigation and sign-out
tasks.description: Create <apps/web/app/candidate/layout.tsx> as a server component. Import `auth` from `@feature/auth`. Render a top navigation bar with a link to /candidate/processes (My Processes). Display the user's email and role badge. Include a `<form action={async () => { 'use server'; await signOut() }}>` with a Sign Out button.

tasks.summary: Create marketer layout with navigation and sign-out
tasks.description: Create <apps/web/app/marketer/layout.tsx>. Navigation links: /marketer/processes (Processes), /marketer/processes/new (New Process). Same user info and sign-out pattern as candidate layout.

tasks.summary: Create supporter layout with navigation and sign-out
tasks.description: Create <apps/web/app/supporter/layout.tsx>. Navigation links: /supporter/sessions (My Sessions). Same user info and sign-out pattern.

tasks.summary: Create admin layout with navigation and sign-out
tasks.description: Create <apps/web/app/admin/layout.tsx>. Navigation links: /admin/processes (Processes), /admin/questions (Question Bank). Same user info and sign-out pattern.

stories.summary: Configure API client singleton with session token attachment and unauthorized handling
stories.description:
Create a configured ApiClient singleton that reads the active NextAuth session JWT and attaches it as a Bearer token on every request. Silent token refresh is handled server-side in the NextAuth jwt callback, so the client always receives a fresh token from getSession(). On a 401 response (session revoked or expired), sign out and redirect to /login. Export the singleton from a central lib/api module.

Key paths: <apps/web/lib/api/client.ts>, <clients/api-client/src/lib/client.ts>

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Every API request includes the Bearer token
acceptanceCriteria.given: a logged-in user triggers a React Query fetch
acceptanceCriteria.then: the Authorization header contains 'Bearer <accessToken>' from the current NextAuth session

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: 401 response clears the session and redirects to login
acceptanceCriteria.given: the backend responds with 401 (e.g., token revoked)
acceptanceCriteria.then: signOut is called and the browser navigates to /login

tasks.summary: Create ApiClient singleton with onAuthenticated and onUnauthorized callbacks
tasks.description: Create <apps/web/lib/api/client.ts>. Instantiate ApiClient with enableRefreshToken: false (refresh is handled by NextAuth JWT callback). In onAuthenticated, call getSession() and set config.headers.Authorization = 'Bearer ' + session.user.jwtToken. In onUnauthorized, call signOut({ redirect: false }) then set window.location.href = '/login'.

tasks.summary: Update env files to include BACKEND_API_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL
tasks.description: Add BACKEND_API_URL=http://localhost:8080 and NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:8080 to <apps/web/.env> and <apps/web/.env.example>. The ApiClient reads BACKEND_API_URL server-side; NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL is available client-side.

tasks.summary: Export apiClient and create typed React Query hook wrappers
tasks.description: Export the singleton from <apps/web/lib/api/index.ts>. Create a thin useQuery wrapper (lib/hooks/use-api-query.ts) that forwards generic API errors to Sonner toast via the existing withToast utility.

stories.summary: Define Zod schemas for all API request and response shapes
stories.description:
Create Zod schemas for every backend DTO used by the frontend. These schemas serve as the single source of truth for request validation (forms) and response parsing (React Query). They must match the camelCase JSON fields returned by the Spring Boot backend.

DTOs to cover:
- Auth: loginRequest, loginResponse, refreshRequest, refreshResponse
- Process: createProcessRequest, updateProcessRequest, interviewProcessResponse
- Session: createSessionRequest, updateSessionRequest, transitionRequest, interviewSessionResponse
- Question: createQuestionRequest, updateQuestionRequest, questionResponse, sessionQuestionResponse, linkQuestionRequest
- Client: createClientRequest, updateClientRequest, clientResponse
- User: createUserRequest, updateUserRequest, userResponse
- Common: pageResponse<T>, statusHistoryEntry

Key paths: <apps/web/lib/schemas/>

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Login form validates client-side before submission
acceptanceCriteria.given: a user submits the login form with an invalid email format
acceptanceCriteria.when: the Zod loginRequest schema is applied
acceptanceCriteria.then: a validation error is shown inline without an API call being made

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: API response is parsed and typed at runtime
acceptanceCriteria.given: the backend returns an interviewProcessResponse payload
acceptanceCriteria.when: the React Query success handler runs
acceptanceCriteria.then: the Zod schema parses the response and TypeScript infers the typed result

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Unexpected API shape triggers a parse error
acceptanceCriteria.given: the backend returns a response missing a required field
acceptanceCriteria.when: the Zod schema parses the response
acceptanceCriteria.then: a ZodError is thrown and the useApiError handler shows a toast

tasks.description: Define loginRequestSchema (email + password), loginResponseSchema (accessToken, refreshToken, role enum, expiresIn), refreshRequestSchema, refreshResponseSchema (accessToken, expiresIn).

tasks.summary: Create process and session schemas in lib/schemas/process.ts
tasks.description: Define createProcessRequestSchema, updateProcessRequestSchema, interviewProcessResponseSchema (UUID fields, ProcessStatus enum, Instant as z.string().datetime()). Define createSessionRequestSchema, updateSessionRequestSchema, transitionRequestSchema, interviewSessionResponseSchema (SessionStatus enum, all UUID and Instant fields).

Define createQuestionRequestSchema, updateQuestionRequestSchema, questionResponseSchema (UUID, clientId, topic, round, body, version, active). Define sessionQuestionResponseSchema and linkQuestionRequestSchema.

tasks.summary: Create user and client schemas in lib/schemas/user.ts and lib/schemas/client.ts
tasks.description: Define createUserRequestSchema, updateUserRequestSchema, userResponseSchema. Define createClientRequestSchema, updateClientRequestSchema, clientResponseSchema.

tasks.summary: Create common schemas and barrel export in lib/schemas/index.ts
tasks.description: Define pageResponseSchema<T> (data array, total, page, limit) as a generic schema factory. Define statusHistoryEntrySchema. Export all schemas from lib/schemas/index.ts.
